skill.name: deployment-pipeline-design
skill.description: Design multi-stage CI/CD pipelines with approval gates, security checks, and deployment orchestration. Use when architecting deployment workflows, setting up continuous delivery, or implementing GitOps practices.
skill.summary: Design secure multi-stage CI/CD pipelines with approvals, deployment strategies, monitoring, and rollback plans.
skill.author: wshobson

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many external command, network, environment, and weak-crypto patterns, but review found they are documentation examples inside SKILL.md. No evidence found of executable code, prompt injection, credential exfiltration, or malicious intent. The remaining risk is low because users may adapt deployment examples that include shell commands, URLs, secrets, and environment variables.

low_findings.title: Documentation Examples Include Deployment Commands
low_findings.description: The static external command findings are false positives for marketplace execution because SKILL.md contains Markdown examples, not runnable skill scripts. The examples still include kubectl, docker, make, npm, curl, jq, and shell control flow that users should review before adapting to real systems.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The referenced lines are fenced Markdown examples and are not invoked by any script in the scanned file. They could affect users only if copied into a real CI/CD environment.

low_findings.title: Placeholder Network and Secret References
low_findings.description: The network and environment findings are example placeholders for health checks, webhook notifications, monitoring endpoints, and feature flag configuration. No evidence found that the skill sends data to external services or reads live credentials during skill execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The suspicious strings are in illustrative configuration snippets using example.com, API_KEY, secrets.SLACK_WEBHOOK, and PROMETHEUS_URL. I did not find executable logic that accesses real secrets or performs network exfiltration.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Finding Is a False Positive
low_findings.description: The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts appear to match words in metadata and CI/CD terminology rather than actual cryptographic code. No evidence found of MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or similar algorithms being implemented or recommended.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines contain front matter, headings, and general purpose text. I did not find cryptographic algorithm usage in those lines.

dangerous_patterns.title: External Commands in Documentation Examples
dangerous_patterns.description: Static analysis detected shell-like commands in fenced examples. They are not executed by the skill, but they should be treated as templates requiring environment-specific review.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The pattern exists, but it is confined to Markdown code blocks. The risk is user misuse, not automatic command execution.

content.value_statement: Release pipelines often fail when approvals, tests, deployment strategies, and rollback paths are designed separately. This skill helps Claude, Codex, and Claude Code produce structured CI/CD designs with gates, verification, and recovery plans.
actual_capabilities: Designs multi-stage deployment flows from source checkout through production verification.
Compares rolling, blue-green, canary, and feature flag release strategies.
Defines manual, timed, and multi-approver production gate patterns.
Adds security scanning, health checks, monitoring, notifications, and rollback steps.
Suggests pipeline metrics such as deployment frequency, lead time, failure rate, and MTTR.
limitations: Provides architecture guidance and examples, not a validated production pipeline for every platform.
Requires users to replace placeholder commands, URLs, secrets, and service names.
Does not inspect a live repository, cloud account, or Kubernetes cluster unless the host assistant has that context.
Cannot guarantee compliance with organization-specific change management rules.

use_cases.title: Plan Production Release Gates
use_cases.description: Create a release flow with staging validation, human approval, production deployment, and post-deployment verification.

use_cases.title: Choose a Deployment Strategy
use_cases.description: Compare rolling, canary, blue-green, and feature flag approaches for risk, cost, rollback speed, and operational fit.

use_cases.title: Standardize CI/CD Practices
use_cases.description: Document repeatable pipeline stages, security checks, monitoring expectations, and rollback procedures for application teams.

prompt_templates.title: Draft a Basic Pipeline
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a simple CI/CD pipeline for my application. Include build, test, staging deploy, approval, production deploy, verification, and rollback stages.

prompt_templates.title: Add Approval Gates
prompt_templates.prompt: Design approval gates for a production deployment pipeline. Include who approves, what evidence they review, timeout behavior, and emergency release handling.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Release Strategies
prompt_templates.prompt: Compare rolling, blue-green, canary, and feature flag deployment strategies for my service. Recommend one strategy and explain the tradeoffs.

prompt_templates.title: Architect a Full Delivery System
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a production-grade deployment pipeline architecture for a Kubernetes service using GitOps. Include security scans, policy gates, monitoring, notifications, rollback automation, and metrics.

output_examples.input: Design a pipeline for a web service that deploys to Kubernetes.
output: A staged flow from source checkout to build, test, staging deployment, integration testing, approval, production deployment, verification, and rollback.
Recommended gates for test results, security scan results, staging health, and production approval.
A deployment strategy recommendation based on traffic risk and rollback requirements.

output_examples.input: Help me decide between canary and blue-green deployment.
output: A tradeoff comparison covering release risk, infrastructure cost, observability needs, rollback speed, and operational complexity.
A clear recommendation for the target service and conditions that would change the recommendation.

output_examples.input: Improve our rollback plan for failed production releases.
output: A rollback workflow with health checks, failure thresholds, notification steps, and manual override points.
Metrics to monitor during and after rollback, including error rate, latency, and deployment status.

best_practices: Provide the target platform, deployment environment, approval policy, and existing tooling before asking for a design.
Ask for rollback and verification steps in the same request as the deployment plan.
Review generated commands, secrets handling, and endpoint names before using examples in a real pipeline.
anti_patterns: Do not copy placeholder URLs, API keys, or service names into production configuration.
Do not skip approval gates for high-risk production releases without a documented emergency process.
Do not treat a sample pipeline as compliant without reviewing organization policy and audit requirements.

faq.question: Can this skill create a full CI/CD pipeline design?
faq.answer: Yes. It can outline stages, gates, deployment strategy, verification, monitoring, and rollback behavior.

faq.question: Does it support GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, and Azure Pipelines?
faq.answer: It includes examples for these systems, but generated output should be adapted to your exact platform.

faq.question: Can it choose between canary, rolling, and blue-green deployments?
faq.answer: Yes. It can compare tradeoffs and recommend a strategy based on risk, cost, traffic, and rollback needs.

faq.question: Does it manage secrets securely?
faq.answer: It recommends secret stores and platform secret references. Users must configure real secrets in approved systems.

faq.question: Will it execute deployment commands?
faq.answer: No. The skill provides guidance and examples. Any commands must be reviewed and run by the user or host tool.

faq.question: Is it suitable for regulated release processes?
faq.answer: It can help document gates and audit evidence, but compliance requirements need organization-specific review.
